findings.one_line: This leaf is not discharged inside the core DAG: its proof is only a citation to external testing inequalities, so the minimax-lower chain still depends on a result that is not represented or reproduced as a core node.

The upper-rate proof uses extra law-side restrictions (`ass:marginal-overlap`, and at `gamma=0` also `ass:strict-overlap-endpoint`) that are not part of `def:law-class`, but the node still quantifies the supremum over `P_class(alpha,gamma)`.

The two-sided bracket is stated for `M_n(alpha,gamma)` over the single target class in `def:minimax-regret`, but the discharged upper half is only established on the stricter subclass used in `oeq:feasible-upper`, so the headline characterization is overstated as written.
